requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall supersede the earlier directions on the subject,
namely, 'Master Circular- 'Classification and Reporting' (DCBR.CO.BPD.MC.No.1/
12.05.001/2015-16) dated July 1, 2015.
requirements.object: on the subject, namely, 'Master Circular- 'Classification and Reporting' (DCBR

requirements.full_sentence:
1.1 Short Title and Commencement
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Fraud Risk
Management in UCBs / StCBs / CCBs) Directions, 2024.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Fraud Risk Management in UCBs / StCBs / CCBs) Directions, 2024

requirements.full_sentence:
Such
Cooperative Banks shall hereinafter collectively be referred to as 'Cooperative
Banks' for the purpose of these Directions.
requirements.object: to as 'Cooperative Banks' for the purpose of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1 There shall be a Board2 Approved Policy3 on fraud risk management delineating
roles and responsibilities of Board / Board Committees and Senior Management of
the Cooperative Bank.The Policy shall also incorporate measures for ensuring
1 StCBs / CCBs shall report the incidents of fraud to NABARD as hitherto.
requirements.object: Policy3 on fraud risk management delineating roles and responsibilities of Board / Board Committees and Senior Management of the

requirements.full_sentence: 2 Board of Directors of a Cooperative Bank 3 The policy shall inter alia contain measures towards prevention, early detection, investigation, staff accountability, monitoring, recovery and reporting of frauds.
requirements.object: towards prevention, early detection, investigation, staff accountability, monitoring, recovery and reporting of frauds

requirements.full_sentence:
compliance with principles of natural justice4 in a time-bound manner which at a
minimum shall include:

The SCN shall provide complete
details of transactions / actions / events basis which declaration and reporting
of a fraud is being contemplated under these Directions.
requirements.object: transactions / actions / events basis which declaration and reporting of a fraud is being contemplated under these Directi

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1.2 A reasonable time of not less than 21 days shall be provided to the
Persons / Entities on whom the SCN was served to respond to the said SCN.
requirements.object: Persons / Entities on whom the SCN was served to respond to the said SCN

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1.3 Cooperative Banks shall have a well laid out system for issuance of
SCN and examination of the responses / submissions made by the Persons /
Entities prior to declaring such Persons / Entities as fraudulent.
requirements.object: out system for issuance of SCN and examination of the responses / submissions made by the Persons / Entities prior to declaring such

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1.4 A reasoned Order shall be served on the Persons / Entities conveying
the decision of the Cooperative Banks regarding declaration / classification of
the account as fraud or otherwise.
requirements.object: Persons / Entities conveying the decision of the Cooperative Banks regarding declaration / classification of the account as fraud or

requirements.full_sentence:
Such Order(s) must contain relevant facts /
circumstances relied upon, the submission made against the SCN and the
reasons for classification as fraud or otherwise.
requirements.object: circumstances relied upon, the submission made against the SCN and the reasons for classification as fraud or otherwise

requirements.full_sentence:
2.2 The Fraud Risk Management Policy shall be reviewed by the Board at least once
in three years, or more frequently, as may be prescribed by the Board.
requirements.object: Board at least once in three years, or more frequently, as may be prescribed by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
2.3.1 Cooperative Banks shall constitute a Committee of the Board to be
known as 'Special Committee of the Board for Monitoring and Follow-up of
cases of Frauds' (SCBMF) with a minimum of three members of the Board,
4 Please refer to the judgement of the Hon'ble Supreme Court dated March 27, 2023 on Civil Appeal No.7300 of 2022 in the matter of State Bank of India & Ors Vs.
requirements.object: the Board to be known as 'Special Committee of the Board for Monitoring and Follow-up of cases of Frauds' (SCBMF) with a min

requirements.full_sentence: and connected matters, read with the Order dated May 12, 2023 passed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court in Misc.
requirements.object: the Hon'ble Supreme Court in Misc

requirements.full_sentence: 12000 of 2021 and connected matters shall be referred to.

6 As non-whole-time directors (like nominee directors and independent directors) are normally not in charge of, or responsible to the company for the conduct of business of the company, Cooperative Banks may take this into consideration before proceeding against such directors under these Directions.
requirements.object: before proceeding against such directors under these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The Committee
shall be headed by one of the directors.
requirements.object: of the directors

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs categorised as Tier 1 & 2 for
regulatory purposes8 and StCBs / CCBs having deposits below ₹1000 crore9,
shall have the option of constituting a Committee of the Executives (CoE) with
a minimum of three members, at least one of whom shall be the Chief
Executive Officer for the purpose of performing the roles and responsibilities
of SCBMF as required under these Directions.
requirements.object: constituting a Committee of the Executives (CoE) with a minimum of three members, at least one of whom shall be the Chief Executive

requirements.full_sentence:
2.3.2 SCBMF shall oversee the effectiveness of the fraud risk management in
the Cooperative Bank.
requirements.object: the fraud risk management in the Cooperative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
SCBMF shall review and monitor the cases of frauds,
including root cause analysis, and suggest mitigating measures for
strengthening the internal controls, risk management framework and
minimising the incidence of frauds.
requirements.object: cases of frauds, including root cause analysis, and suggest mitigating measures for strengthening the internal controls, risk m

requirements.full_sentence:
The coverage10 and periodicity of such
reviews shall be decided by the Board of the Cooperative Bank.
requirements.object: Board of the Cooperative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
2.4 The Senior Management shall be responsible for implementation of the fraud risk
management policy approved by the Board of the Cooperative Bank.
requirements.object: of the fraud risk management policy approved by the Board of the Cooperative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
A periodic
review of incidents of fraud shall also be placed before Board / Audit Committee of
Board (ACB), as appropriate, by the Senior Management of the Cooperative Bank.
requirements.object: Board / Audit Committee of Board (ACB), as appropriate, by the Senior Management of the Cooperative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
2.5 Cooperative Banks shall put in place a transparent mechanism to ensure that
Whistle Blower complaints on possible fraud cases / suspicious activities in
account(s) are examined and concluded appropriately under their Whistle Blower
Policy.
requirements.object: transparent mechanism to ensure that Whistle Blower complaints on possible fraud cases / suspicious activities in account(s) are examin

requirements.full_sentence:
2.6 Cooperative Banks shall set-up an appropriate organisational structure for
institutionalisation of fraud risk management11 within their overall risk management
functions / Department.
requirements.object: structure for institutionalisation of fraud risk management11 within their overall risk management functions / De

requirements.full_sentence:
A sufficiently senior official shall be responsible for
monitoring and reporting of frauds.
requirements.object: and reporting of frauds

requirements.full_sentence: 9 The deposits of StCBs and CCBs shall be reckoned as per audited balance sheet as on 31st March of the immediate preceding Financial Year.
requirements.object: audited balance sheet as on 31st March of the immediate preceding Financial Year

requirements.full_sentence: 10 The coverage may include, among others, categories/trends of frauds, industry/sectoral/ geographical concentration of frauds, delay in detection/classification of frauds and delay in examination/conclusion of staff accountability, etc.
requirements.object: of frauds, industry/sectoral/ geographical concentration of frauds, delay in detection/classification of frau

requirements.full_sentence:
Applicable Cooperative Banks for the purpose of this
Chapter) shall have a framework for Early Warning Signals (EWS) under the
overall Fraud Risk Management Policy approved by the Board.
requirements.object: Early Warning Signals (EWS) under the overall Fraud Risk Management Policy approved by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
3.1.2 A Board Level Committee13 shall oversee the effectiveness of the
framework for EWS.
requirements.object: the framework for EWS

requirements.full_sentence:
The Senior Management shall be responsible for
implementation of a robust Framework for EWS within the Applicable
Cooperative Bank.
requirements.object: of a robust Framework for EWS within the Applicable Cooperative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
3.1.3 Applicable Cooperative Banks shall identify appropriate early warning
indicators for monitoring credit facilities / loan accounts and other banking
transactions.
requirements.object: indicators for monitoring credit facilities / loan accounts and other banking transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
These indicators shall be reviewed periodically for their
effectiveness.
requirements.object: their effectiveness

requirements.full_sentence:
Suspicion of fraudulent activity thrown up by the presence of
one or more EWS indicators shall alert / trigger deeper investigation from
potential fraud angle and initiating preventive measures.
requirements.object: investigation from potential fraud angle and initiating preventive measures

requirements.full_sentence:
3.1.4 The EWS framework shall be subject to suitable validation in
accordance with the directions of the Board Level Committee so as to ensure
its integrity, robustness and consistency of the outcomes.
requirements.object: validation in accordance with the directions of the Board Level Committee so as to ensure its integrity, robustness and consist

requirements.full_sentence: 3.2 The EWS Framework shall provide for, among others:

3.3.1 The EWS system shall be comprehensive and designed to include both
the quantitative and qualitative indicators to make the framework robust and
effective.
requirements.object: to include both the quantitative and qualitative indicators to make the framework robust and effective

requirements.full_sentence:
The broad indicators which the EWS system may illustratively
12 The Directions under Chapter III shall be applicable to UCBs categorised as Tier 3 & 4 for regulatory purposes and StCBs / CCBs having deposits above ₹1000 crore only.
requirements.object: categorised as Tier 3 & 4 for regulatory purposes and StCBs / CCBs having deposits above ₹1000 crore only

requirements.full_sentence:
3.3.2 Generation of EWS alert(s) / trigger(s) shall necessitate examination
whether the account needs to be investigated from potential fraud angle.
requirements.object: account needs to be investigated from potential fraud angle

requirements.full_sentence:
3.4.1 Applicable Cooperative Banks shall develop / strengthen their EWS
system by identifying suitable indicators and parameterising them in their
EWS system for monitoring other banking / non-credit related transactions.
requirements.object: EWS system by identifying suitable indicators and parameterising them in their EWS system for monitoring other banking / no

requirements.full_sentence:
Applicable Cooperative Banks shall strive to continuously upgrade the EWS
system for enhancing its integrity and robustness, monitor other banking /
non-credit related transactions efficiently and prevent fraudulent activities
through the banking channel.
requirements.object: the EWS system for enhancing its integrity and robustness, monitor other banking / non-credit related transactions effi

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the effectiveness of EWS system shall
be tested periodically.

3.4.2 The design and specification of EWS system shall be robust and
resilient to ensure that integrity of system is maintained, personal and
financial data of customers are secure and transaction monitoring for
prevention / detection of potential fraud is on real-time basis15.
requirements.object: to ensure that integrity of system is maintained, personal and financial data of customers are secure and transaction monitori

requirements.full_sentence:
Applicable
Cooperative Banks shall remain vigilant in monitoring transactions / unusual
activities, specifically in the non-KYC compliant and money mule accounts
etc., so as to contain unauthorised / fraudulent transactions and to prevent
misuse of banking / financial channel.
requirements.object: transactions / unusual activities, specifically in the non-KYC compliant and money mule accounts etc

requirements.full_sentence:
3.4.3 The dedicated MIS Unit or other Analytics Setup in Applicable
Cooperative Banks shall extensively monitor and analyse financial
transactions, including transactions carried out through digital platforms /
applications, in order to identify unusual patterns and activities which could
alert the Applicable Cooperative Banks in time for initiating appropriate
measures towards prevention of fraudulent activities.
requirements.object: financial transactions, including transactions carried out through digital platforms / applications, in order to ident

requirements.full_sentence:
3.5 Applicable Cooperative Banks shall put in place / suitably upgrade their existing
EWS system within six months from the date of issuance of these Directions.
requirements.object: suitably upgrade their existing EWS system within six months from the date of issuance of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Credit facility / Loan account / Other banking transactions - Indication of fraudulent activities
Cooperative Banks shall monitor activities in credit facility / loan account / other
banking transactions and remain alert on activities which could potentially turn out to
be fraudulent.
requirements.object: facility / loan account / other banking transactions and remain alert on activities which could potentially turn out to b

conditions:
where there is a suspicion / indication of wrongdoing or fraudulent
activity
requirements.full_sentence:
4.1 In cases where there is a suspicion / indication of wrongdoing or fraudulent
activity, Cooperative Banks shall use an external audit16 or an internal audit as per
their Board Approved Policy for further investigation in such accounts.
requirements.object: or an internal audit as per their Board Approved Policy for further investigation in such accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
4.1.1 Cooperative Banks shall frame a policy on engagement of external
auditors covering aspects such as due diligence, competency and track
record of the auditors, among others.
requirements.object: engagement of external auditors covering aspects such as due diligence, competency and track record of the auditors, among others

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the contractual agreement with
the auditors shall, inter alia, contain suitable clauses on timeline for
completion of the audit and submission of audit report to the Cooperative
Banks within a specified time limit, as approved by the Board.
requirements.object: suitable clauses on timeline for completion of the audit and submission of audit report to the Cooperative Banks within a specif

requirements.full_sentence:
4.1.2 The loan agreement with the borrower shall contain clauses for conduct
of such audit at the behest of lender(s).
requirements.object: of such audit at the behest of lender(s)

conditions:
where the audit report
submitted remains inconclusive or is delayed due to non-cooperation by the
borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where the audit report
submitted remains inconclusive or is delayed due to non-cooperation by the
borrower, Cooperative Banks shall conclude on status of the account as a
fraud or otherwise based on the material available on their record and their
own internal investigation / assessment in such cases17.
requirements.object: the account as a fraud or otherwise based on the material available on their record and their own internal investigation / asses

requirements.full_sentence:
4.1.3 Cooperative Banks (sole lending, multiple banking arrangement or
consortium lending) shall ensure that the principles of natural justice18 are
strictly adhered to before classifying / declaring an account as fraud.
requirements.object: of natural justice18 are strictly adhered to before classifying / declaring an account as fraud

requirements.full_sentence: 17 Cooperative Banks shall ensure that principles of natural justice are strictly adhered to before classifying / declaring an account as fraud (Please refer to Para 2.1 ibid).
requirements.object: natural justice are strictly adhered to before classifying / declaring an account as fraud (Please refer to Para 2

requirements.full_sentence: 12000 of 2021 and connected matters shall be referred to (Please refer to Para 2.1 ibid).
requirements.object: refer to Para 2

conditions: In case an account is identified as a fraud by any Cooperative Bank
requirements.full_sentence:
4.1.4 In case an account is identified as a fraud by any Cooperative Bank, the
borrowal accounts of other group companies, in which one or more
promoter(s) / whole-time director(s) are common shall also be subjected to
examination by Cooperative Banks concerned from fraud angle under these
Directions.
requirements.object: examination by Cooperative Banks concerned from fraud angle under these Directions

conditions:
where Law Enforcement Agencies (LEAs) have suo moto
initiated investigation involving a borrower account
requirements.full_sentence:
4.1.5 In cases where Law Enforcement Agencies (LEAs) have suo moto
initiated investigation involving a borrower account, Cooperative Banks shall
follow the process of classification of account as fraud as per their Board
approved Policy and in tune with the process as given under Para 2.1 ibid.
requirements.object: classification of account as fraud as per their Board approved Policy and in tune with the process as given under Para 2

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, Cooperative Banks
may incorporate necessary terms and conditions in their agreements with third-party
service providers to hold them accountable in situations where wilful negligence /
malpractice by them is found to be a causative factor for fraud.
requirements.object: conditions in their agreements with third-party service providers to hold them accountable in situations where wilful

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3.1 Cooperative Banks shall initiate and complete the examination of staff
accountability in all fraud cases in a time-bound manner in accordance with
their internal policy.
requirements.object: examination of staff accountability in all fraud cases in a time-bound manner in accordance with their internal policy

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3.2 In cases involving very senior executives of the Cooperative Banks (MD
& CEO / Chief Executive Officer / Executives of equivalent rank)19, the ACB
shall initiate examination of their accountability and place before the Board.
requirements.object: accountability and place before the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
4.4.1 Persons / Entities classified and reported as fraud by Cooperative Banks
and also Entities and Persons associated20 with such Entities, shall be
19 Such executive shall not participate in the meeting of the Board / ACB / SCBMF in which their accountability is to be considered.
requirements.object: of the Board / ACB / SCBMF in which their accountability is to be considered

conditions: in case of a natural person
requirements.full_sentence: (b) in case of a natural person, all entities in which she / he is associated as promoter, or director, or as one in charge and responsible for the management of the affairs of the entity shall be deemed to be associated.
requirements.object: associated

requirements.full_sentence:
4.4.2 Lending to such Persons / Entities, being commercial decisions, the
lending Cooperative Banks shall have the sole discretion to entertain or
decline such requests for credit facilities after the expiry of the mandatory
cooling period as mentioned at Para 4.4.1 above.
requirements.object: to entertain or decline such requests for credit facilities after the expiry of the mandatory cooling period as mentioned at

requirements.full_sentence:
4.5.1 In case an entity classified as fraud has subsequently undergone a
resolution either under IBC or under the resolution framework of RBI21
resulting in a change in the management and control of the entity / business
enterprise, the Cooperative Bank shall examine whether the entity shall
continue to remain classified as fraud or the classification as fraud could be
removed after implementation of the Resolution Plan under IBC or aforesaid
prudential framework.
requirements.object: shall continue to remain classified as fraud or the classification as fraud could be removed after implementation of the Re

requirements.full_sentence:
4.5.2 The penal measures as detailed in Para 4.4 shall not be applicable to
entities / business enterprises after implementation of the resolution plan
under IBC or aforesaid prudential framework.
requirements.object: / business enterprises after implementation of the resolution plan under IBC or aforesaid prudential framework

requirements.full_sentence:
4.5.3 The penal measures detailed in Para 4.4 shall continue to apply to the
erstwhile promoter(s)/ director(s)/ persons who were in charge and
responsible for the management of the affairs of the entity / business
enterprise.
requirements.object: the erstwhile promoter(s)/ director(s)/ persons who were in charge and responsible for the management of the affairs of the entit

requirements.full_sentence:
5.1 Cooperative Banks shall immediately report the incidents of fraud to appropriate
LEAs viz.
requirements.object: of fraud to appropriate LEAs viz

requirements.full_sentence:
5.2 Cooperative Banks shall establish suitable nodal point(s) / designate officer(s) for
reporting incidents of fraud to LEAs and for proper coordination to meet the
requirements of the LEAs.
requirements.object: / designate officer(s) for reporting incidents of fraud to LEAs and for proper coordination to meet the requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
6.1 Reporting of Incidents of Fraud to Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
To ensure uniformity and consistency while reporting incidents of fraud to RBI
through Fraud Monitoring Returns (FMRs) using online portal, UCBs shall choose
the most appropriate category from any one of the following:
requirements.object: category from any one of the following:

requirements.full_sentence: They shall report incidents of fraud to NABARD in the manner and in Returns / Formats as prescribed by NABARD.
requirements.object: to NABARD in the manner and in Returns / Formats as prescribed by NABARD

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2.1 UCBs shall furnish FMR23 in individual fraud cases, irrespective of the
amount involved, immediately but not later than 14 days from the date of
classification24 of an incident / account as fraud.
requirements.object: fraud cases, irrespective of the amount involved, immediately but not later than 14 days from the date of classification24

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2.2 UCBs shall adhere to the timeframe prescribed in these Master
Directions for reporting of fraud cases to RBI25.
requirements.object: prescribed in these Master Directions for reporting of fraud cases to RBI25

requirements.full_sentence:
UCBs must examine and fix
staff accountability for delays in identification of fraud cases and in reporting
to RBI.
requirements.object: accountability for delays in identification of fraud cases and in reporting to RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2.3 While reporting frauds, UCBs shall ensure that persons / entities who /
which are not involved / associated with the fraud are not reported in the
FMR.
requirements.object: entities who / which are not involved / associated with the fraud are not reported in the FMR

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2.4 UCBs may, under exceptional circumstances, withdraw FMR / remove
name(s) of perpetrator(s) from FMR.
requirements.object: withdraw FMR / remove name(s) of perpetrator(s) from FMR

requirements.full_sentence:
Such withdrawal / removal shall,
however, be made with due justification and with the approval of an official at
least in the rank of a director.
requirements.object: with due justification and with the approval of an official at least in the rank of a director

requirements.full_sentence:
6.3.1 UCBs shall close fraud cases using 'Closure Module' where the actions
as stated below are complete:
requirements.object: 'Closure Module' where the actions as stated below are complete:

requirements.full_sentence:
6.3.2 In all closure cases of reported frauds, UCBs shall maintain details of
such cases for examination by auditors.
requirements.object: cases for examination by auditors

requirements.full_sentence:
7.1 To ensure uniformity and avoid duplication, reporting of frauds involving forged
instruments, including fake / forged instruments sent in clearing in respect of
truncated instruments, shall continue to be done by the paying banker and not by the
23 Updates to the FMR shall be provided through FMR Update Application (FUA).
requirements.object: by the paying banker and not by the 23 Updates to the FMR shall be provided through FMR Update Application (FUA)

requirements.full_sentence:
In such cases the presenting Cooperative Bank shall immediately
handover the underlying instrument to the drawee / paying Cooperative Bank, as and
when demanded, to enable them to inform LEAs for investigation and further action
under law and to report the fraud to RBI.
requirements.object: instrument to the drawee / paying Cooperative Bank, as and when demanded, to enable them to inform LEAs for invest

requirements.full_sentence:
or where the
amount has been credited before realisation and subsequently the instrument is
found to be fake / forged and returned by the paying Cooperative Bank, the
presenting Cooperative Bank which is defrauded or is put to loss by paying the
amount before realisation of the instrument shall file the fraud report with the RBI
and inform the LEAs for investigation and further action under law.
requirements.object: with the RBI and inform the LEAs for investigation and further action under law

requirements.full_sentence:
8.1 Legal Audit of Title Documents in respect of Large Value Loan Accounts
Cooperative Banks shall subject the title deeds and other related title documents in
respect of all credit facilities of ₹1 crore and above to periodic legal audit and re-
verification, till the loan is fully repaid.
requirements.object: and other related title documents in respect of all credit facilities of ₹1 crore and above to periodic legal audit and re- ve

requirements.full_sentence:
The scope and periodicity of legal audit shall
be in accordance with the Board approved policy referred to in clause 2.1 above.
requirements.object: the Board approved policy referred to in clause 2

requirements.full_sentence:
8.2 Treatment of Accounts classified as Fraud and sold to other Lenders / Asset Reconstruction Companies (ARCs)27
Cooperative Banks shall complete the investigation from fraud angle before
transferring the loan account / credit facility to other lenders / ARCs.
requirements.object: fraud angle before transferring the loan account / credit facility to other lenders / ARCs

requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where
Cooperative Banks conclude that a fraud has been perpetrated in the account, they
shall report it to RBI / NABARD28 before selling the asset to other lenders / ARCs29.
requirements.object: / NABARD28 before selling the asset to other lenders / ARCs29

requirements.full_sentence:
8.3.1 During the course of the audit, auditors may come across instances
where the transactions in the account or the documents point to the possibility
of fraudulent transactions in the account.
requirements.object: the transactions in the account or the documents point to the possibility of fraudulent transactions in the account

requirements.full_sentence: 28 StCBs / CCBs shall report to NABARD.

conditions: where accounts are sold to ARCs
requirements.full_sentence: 29 In cases where accounts are sold to ARCs, Cooperative Banks shall continue to report subsequent developments in such accounts to RBI / NABARD, by obtaining requisite information periodically from the concerned ARCs.
requirements.object: developments in such accounts to RBI / NABARD, by obtaining requisite information periodically from the concerned ARCs

requirements.full_sentence:
should immediately bring it to the notice of the senior management and if
necessary, to the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) of the Cooperative
Banks for appropriate action.
requirements.object: the notice of the senior management and if necessary, to the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) of the Cooperative Banks for a

requirements.full_sentence:
8.3.2 Internal Audit in Cooperative Banks shall cover controls and processes
involved in prevention, detection, classification, monitoring, reporting, closure
and withdrawal of fraud cases, and also weaknesses observed in the critical
processes in the fraud risk management framework of the Cooperative
Bank30.
requirements.object: involved in prevention, detection, classification, monitoring, reporting, closure and withdrawal of fraud cases, and also

conditions:
when the
fraud came to light in the concerned branch / audit / department
requirements.full_sentence:
8.4.2 The 'date of detection' to be reported in FMR is the actual date when the
fraud came to light in the concerned branch / audit / department, as the case
may be, and not the date of approval by the competent authority of the
Cooperative Bank.
requirements.object: date of approval by the competent authority of the Cooperative Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
9.1 Cooperative Banks shall report32 instances of theft, burglary, dacoity and robbery
(including attempted cases), to Fraud Monitoring Group (FMG), Department of
Supervision, Central Office, Reserve Bank of India, immediately (not later than seven
days) from their occurrence.
requirements.object: burglary, dacoity and robbery (including attempted cases), to Fraud Monitoring Group (FMG), Department of Supervision, Ce

requirements.full_sentence: They shall report incidents of theft, burglary, dacoity and robbery to NABARD in the manner and in Returns / Formats as prescribed by NABARD.
requirements.object: burglary, dacoity and robbery to NABARD in the manner and in Returns / Formats as prescribed by NABARD

requirements.full_sentence:
9.2 Cooperative Banks shall also submit a quarterly Return (RBR) on theft, burglary,
dacoity and robbery to RBI using online portal, covering all such cases during the
quarter.
requirements.object: Return (RBR) on theft, burglary, dacoity and robbery to RBI using online portal, covering all such cases during the quarter

requirements.timeline:
within 15 days from the end of the quarter to which it
relates
requirements.full_sentence:
This shall be submitted within 15 days from the end of the quarter to which it
relates.
requirements.object: days from the end of the quarter to which it relates

requirements.full_sentence:
All the
instructions / guidelines contained in these Circulars shall be deemed as given under
these Directions.
requirements.object: under these Directions
